behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between HTTP handler, signature verification, and queue forwarding (MOCK-MISS yes at module boundary). REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess required; in-process request simulation is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text only requires reject/forward logic, not real network conditions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with an in-process HTTP test harness using the real signature middleware and a spy/fake queue module. Send a body with an invalid `X-Signature-256`, assert `401`, and assert queue `enqueue` was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about ordering across modules (handler response path vs persistent queue write), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS yes). REAL-INFRA: can be validated in-process with real queue module (e.g., local DB/file-backed queue) and HTTP harness; no external network/subprocess required. Rejected System because text does not require real network/OS primitives beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue implementation in a local test environment and an in-process HTTP request. Instrument enqueue completion and response send timestamps/order; assert enqueue commit happens before `200` is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration boundary between request handler, queue write, and async downstream processor. Unit may miss real await/Promise wiring mistakes. REAL-INFRA: no need for real network or subprocess; in-process harness with controlled slow processor reproduces failure. Rejected System because behavior text is about not awaiting downstream completion, not OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker module in-process. Make downstream processing block (deferred promise), send valid request, assert response completes promptly while downstream promise remains unresolved, then resolve downstream later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency; real scheduling/timing effects and server runtime behavior are the failure boundary (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process pure mocks can hide event-loop starvation/backpressure issues (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration because the text requires real-time SLA under concurrent load, which is an OS/runtime/system property.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process, generate concurrent traffic where earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work, and measure end-to-end HTTP response times with wall-clock timers. Assert new webhook acknowledgments stay under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at module/state boundary (handler + idempotency store/queue persistence). Unit tests of a single function can miss persistence/constraint wiring issues (MOCK-MISS yes). REAL-INFRA: can be tested with local in-process modules and persistent store; no external network/subprocess required. Rejected System because behavior text does not require cross-process/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler with real dedupe/persistence path in a local integration setup. Submit the same event ID twice and assert first is persisted/enqueued, second is acknowledged per contract but not enqueued again (queue write count remains 1).

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This is a contract translation across modules: queue-full signal to HTTP status/header. Unit is likely too low because failure occurs at handoff mapping. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; queue-full can be induced in-process. Rejected System because the behavior text keys on queue-reported state and response mapping, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue module wired in-process and force a deterministic `queue full` error/result. Assert HTTP `503` and presence/value format of `Retry-After`, and assert event is not accepted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is within queue implementation + persistence layer interaction (storage writes, count/limit enforcement). Unit can miss real persistence semantics. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is sufficient and fits Integration; no real external API/subprocess required. Rejected System because behavior text does not require OS-level events or network behavior, only queue persistence correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a small configured capacity in a local integration environment, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more. Assert overflow is rejected with the queue’s full error and persisted depth never exceeds configured capacity.
